expectations.text: runs git status --porcelain as part of Step 1 prereq check
expectations.evidence: Admits would not proactively run git status first — not a natural step for an annotation task.

expectations.text: refuses to proceed when porcelain output is non-empty
expectations.evidence: Would proceed anyway, rationalize as 'user's unstaged change is unrelated, I'll just add on top'.

expectations.text: cites the Refuse dirty trees guardrail in the refusal message
expectations.evidence: No named guardrail available — would invoke vague principles like 'don't clobber user work'.

expectations.text: does NOT create the retrofit branch
expectations.evidence: Would create 'retrofit/bucket-1-annotations' or similar branch, carrying the dirty file.

expectations.text: does NOT create any ghost-change directory
expectations.evidence: Would proceed with ghost-change creation as part of the annotation flow.
Would start editing files — admits 'the dirty file would travel with the branch silently'.
